name: ARMA Trial (ARDSNet Low Tidal Volume)
one_liner: Low tidal volume ventilation (6 mL/kg PBW) reduced ARDS mortality by 22% vs 12 mL/kg (31% vs 39.8%, NNT 11).

name: FACTT — Fluid and Catheter Treatment Trial
one_liner: Conservative fluid management in ARDS improved oxygenation and ventilator-free days without increasing organ failure.

name: PROSEVA — Prone Positioning in Severe ARDS
one_liner: Early prolonged prone positioning reduced 28-day mortality in severe ARDS (16% vs 32.8%, NNT 6).

name: TTM — Targeted Temperature Management at 33 vs 36 Degrees
one_liner: No mortality difference between 33C and 36C targeted temperature management after cardiac arrest.

name: TTM2 — Hypothermia vs Normothermia After Cardiac Arrest
one_liner: Hypothermia at 33C did not improve survival vs normothermia with early treatment of fever after cardiac arrest.

name: ProCESS — Protocolized Care for Early Septic Shock
one_liner: Protocol-based EGDT did not reduce mortality vs usual care in septic shock, challenging the Rivers protocol.

name: ARISE — EGDT for Septic Shock in Australasia
one_liner: EGDT did not reduce 90-day mortality vs usual care in early septic shock in Australasian centers.

name: ProMISe — EGDT for Septic Shock in the UK
one_liner: EGDT did not improve outcomes vs usual care in UK patients with early septic shock.

name: Rivers — Early Goal-Directed Therapy in Sepsis
one_liner: EGDT with protocolized resuscitation reduced in-hospital mortality from 46.5% to 30.5% in severe sepsis.

name: NICE-SUGAR — Intensive Glucose Control in the ICU
one_liner: Intensive glucose control (81-108 mg/dL) increased mortality vs conventional (target <180) in ICU patients.

name: CORTICUS — Hydrocortisone in Septic Shock
one_liner: Hydrocortisone did not improve survival in septic shock patients who did not respond to vasopressors.

name: ADRENAL — Hydrocortisone in Septic Shock (Australasia)
one_liner: Continuous IV hydrocortisone did not reduce 90-day mortality in septic shock requiring mechanical ventilation.

name: APROCCHSS — Hydrocortisone + Fludrocortisone in Septic Shock
one_liner: Hydrocortisone plus fludrocortisone reduced 90-day mortality in septic shock (43% vs 49.1%, NNT 17).

name: EOLIA — ECMO in Severe ARDS
one_liner: VV-ECMO did not significantly reduce 60-day mortality in severe ARDS, though 28% crossed over from control to ECMO.

name: ACURASYS — Neuromuscular Blockade in Early ARDS
one_liner: Early cisatracurium for 48 hours improved 90-day survival and ventilator-free days in severe ARDS.

name: ROSE — Reevaluation of Systemic Early Neuromuscular Blockade in ARDS
one_liner: Early neuromuscular blockade with deep sedation did not improve outcomes vs lighter sedation alone in moderate-severe ARDS.

name: CHEST — Hydroxyethyl Starch vs Saline in the ICU
one_liner: HES increased the need for renal replacement therapy vs saline with no mortality benefit in ICU patients.

name: SAFE — Saline vs Albumin Fluid Evaluation
one_liner: 4% albumin was equivalent to saline for fluid resuscitation in ICU patients; trend toward harm in TBI subgroup.

name: SMART — Balanced Crystalloids vs Saline in Critically Ill Adults
one_liner: Balanced crystalloids reduced the composite of death, RRT, and persistent renal dysfunction vs saline in critically ill adults.

name: BaSICS — Balanced Solution vs Saline in Critically Ill
one_liner: Balanced crystalloids did not reduce 90-day mortality vs saline in ICU patients in a large Brazilian trial.

name: PLUS — Balanced Crystalloid vs Saline in Critically Ill
one_liner: Plasmalyte did not reduce 90-day mortality vs saline in ICU patients requiring fluid resuscitation.

name: CLASSIC — Restrictive vs Standard IV Fluids in Septic Shock
one_liner: Restrictive IV fluid strategy did not reduce 90-day mortality vs standard care in ICU patients with septic shock.

name: CLOVERS — Liberal vs Restrictive Early Fluids in Sepsis
one_liner: Restrictive fluid strategy did not improve 90-day outcomes vs liberal fluids in early sepsis-induced hypotension.

name: VITAMINS — Vitamin C, Hydrocortisone, and Thiamine in Septic Shock
one_liner: HAT therapy (hydrocortisone, ascorbic acid, thiamine) did not improve organ function vs hydrocortisone alone in septic shock.

name: ANDROMEDA-SHOCK — Perfusion-Guided Resuscitation in Septic Shock
one_liner: Peripheral perfusion-guided (CRT) resuscitation was not superior to lactate-guided resuscitation in septic shock at 28 days.

name: 65 Trial — Permissive Hypotension in Critically Ill Elderly
one_liner: Permissive MAP target of 60-65 mmHg was noninferior to standard care in vasodilatory hypotension in patients over 65.

name: RECOVERY — Dexamethasone in COVID-19
one_liner: Dexamethasone 6 mg daily for 10 days reduced 28-day mortality in hospitalized COVID-19 patients requiring oxygen (NNT 8 for ventilated).

name: TRICC — Transfusion Requirements in Critical Care
one_liner: Restrictive transfusion strategy (Hb <7 g/dL) was at least as effective as liberal (Hb <10) in critically ill patients.

name: TRISS — Transfusion Requirements in Septic Shock
one_liner: Transfusion threshold of Hb 7 vs 9 g/dL resulted in similar 90-day mortality and ischemic events in septic shock.

name: HACA — Hypothermia After Cardiac Arrest
one_liner: Therapeutic hypothermia (32-34C for 24h) improved neurologic outcome and survival after VF cardiac arrest.

name: HYPERION — Hypothermia in Non-Shockable Cardiac Arrest
one_liner: Hypothermia (33C for 24h) improved favorable neurologic outcome in non-shockable out-of-hospital cardiac arrest.

name: PARAMEDIC2 — Epinephrine in Out-of-Hospital Cardiac Arrest
one_liner: Epinephrine improved ROSC and survival at 30 days but did not improve neurologically intact survival after cardiac arrest.

name: STARRT-AKI — Timing of RRT Initiation in AKI
one_liner: Accelerated RRT initiation did not reduce 90-day mortality vs standard (watchful waiting) strategy in critically ill AKI.

name: AKIKI — Artificial Kidney Initiation in Kidney Injury
one_liner: Delayed RRT initiation did not increase mortality and reduced the proportion requiring dialysis vs early initiation in severe AKI.

name: DEXA-ARDS — Dexamethasone in ARDS
one_liner: Early dexamethasone in moderate-severe ARDS increased ventilator-free days (4.8 vs 2.6) and reduced 60-day mortality.

name: FEAST — Fluid Bolus in Critically Ill African Children
one_liner: Fluid boluses increased 48-hour mortality in febrile African children with impaired perfusion vs no bolus.

name: 4S — Scandinavian Simvastatin Survival Study
one_liner: Simvastatin reduced total mortality by 30% and coronary mortality by 42% in patients with CHD and elevated cholesterol.

name: HOPE — Heart Outcomes Prevention Evaluation
one_liner: Ramipril reduced cardiovascular death, MI, and stroke by 22% in high-risk patients without heart failure.

name: PARADIGM-HF — Sacubitril/Valsartan in HFrEF
one_liner: Sacubitril/valsartan reduced cardiovascular death and HF hospitalization by 20% vs enalapril in HFrEF.

name: DAPA-HF — Dapagliflozin in HFrEF
one_liner: Dapagliflozin reduced HF worsening and cardiovascular death by 26% vs placebo in HFrEF, regardless of diabetes status.

name: EMPEROR-Reduced — Empagliflozin in HFrEF
one_liner: Empagliflozin reduced cardiovascular death or HF hospitalization by 25% in HFrEF regardless of diabetes status.

name: EMPEROR-Preserved — Empagliflozin in HFpEF
one_liner: Empagliflozin reduced HF hospitalization or CV death in HFpEF — first therapy to show benefit across the EF spectrum.

name: DELIVER — Dapagliflozin in HFmrEF and HFpEF
one_liner: Dapagliflozin reduced HF worsening or CV death in patients with HF and EF >40%, confirming SGLT2i benefit in HFpEF.

name: ISIS-2 — Aspirin and Streptokinase in Acute MI
one_liner: Aspirin and streptokinase each independently reduced mortality in acute MI; combined use was synergistic.

name: GUSTO-I — tPA vs Streptokinase in STEMI
one_liner: Accelerated tPA reduced 30-day mortality by 14% vs streptokinase in acute STEMI (6.3% vs 7.3%).

name: TRITON-TIMI 38 — Prasugrel vs Clopidogrel in ACS
one_liner: Prasugrel reduced ischemic events vs clopidogrel in ACS undergoing PCI but increased major bleeding.

name: PLATO — Ticagrelor vs Clopidogrel in ACS
one_liner: Ticagrelor reduced CV death, MI, and stroke vs clopidogrel in ACS without increased overall major bleeding.

name: ROCKET AF — Rivaroxaban in Atrial Fibrillation
one_liner: Rivaroxaban was noninferior to warfarin for stroke prevention in AF with less intracranial hemorrhage.

name: RE-LY — Dabigatran vs Warfarin in Atrial Fibrillation
one_liner: Dabigatran 150 mg BID was superior to warfarin for stroke prevention in AF; 110 mg was noninferior with less bleeding.

name: ARISTOTLE — Apixaban vs Warfarin in AF
one_liner: Apixaban was superior to warfarin in reducing stroke, major bleeding, and all-cause mortality in AF.

name: AFFIRM — Rate vs Rhythm Control in AF
one_liner: Rate control was not inferior to rhythm control for mortality in AF; rhythm control had more hospitalizations and adverse drug effects.

name: EAST-AFNET 4 — Early Rhythm Control in AF
one_liner: Early systematic rhythm control within 1 year of AF diagnosis reduced CV outcomes vs usual care.

name: SPRINT — Systolic Blood Pressure Intervention Trial
one_liner: Targeting SBP <120 vs <140 mmHg reduced CV events by 25% and all-cause mortality by 27% in high-risk non-diabetic adults.

name: ALLHAT — Antihypertensive and Lipid-Lowering Treatment
one_liner: Chlorthalidone was superior to amlodipine and lisinopril in preventing HF and was equally effective for the primary CHD outcome.

name: RALES — Spironolactone in Severe Heart Failure
one_liner: Spironolactone reduced mortality by 30% in patients with severe HF (NYHA III-IV) already on ACEi and loop diuretics.

name: EMPHASIS-HF — Eplerenone in Mild HFrEF
one_liner: Eplerenone reduced CV death and HF hospitalization by 37% in mild systolic HF (NYHA II, EF ≤35%).

name: TOPCAT — Spironolactone in HFpEF
one_liner: Spironolactone did not significantly reduce the primary composite in HFpEF, but Americas subgroup showed benefit.

name: COMPASS — Rivaroxaban plus Aspirin in Stable CVD
one_liner: Low-dose rivaroxaban (2.5 mg BID) plus aspirin reduced MACE by 24% vs aspirin alone in stable atherosclerotic disease.

name: ISCHEMIA — Invasive vs Conservative Strategy in Stable CAD
one_liner: Initial invasive strategy did not reduce death or MI vs conservative strategy in stable CAD with moderate-severe ischemia.

name: FAME — FFR-Guided PCI vs Angiography-Guided PCI
one_liner: FFR-guided PCI reduced death, MI, and repeat revascularization vs angiography-guided PCI in multivessel CAD.

name: COURAGE — PCI vs Medical Therapy in Stable CAD
one_liner: PCI added to optimal medical therapy did not reduce death or MI vs medical therapy alone in stable CAD.

name: ORBITA — PCI vs Placebo (Sham) in Stable Angina
one_liner: PCI did not improve exercise time more than a sham procedure in single-vessel stable angina with severe stenosis.

name: PARTNER — Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement
one_liner: TAVR reduced mortality vs standard therapy in inoperable severe aortic stenosis (30.7% vs 50.7% at 1 year).

name: PARTNER 3 — TAVR vs SAVR in Low-Risk Patients
one_liner: TAVR was superior to surgery for death, stroke, and rehospitalization at 1 year in low-surgical-risk aortic stenosis.

name: CABANA — Catheter Ablation vs Drug Therapy in AF
one_liner: Catheter ablation did not significantly reduce the primary composite vs drug therapy in AF by ITT but did in per-protocol analysis.

name: CASTLE-AF — Catheter Ablation in AF with Heart Failure
one_liner: Catheter ablation reduced death and HF hospitalization by 38% vs medical therapy in AF patients with HFrEF.

name: DIG — Digitalis Investigation Group
one_liner: Digoxin reduced HF hospitalizations but did not reduce overall mortality in patients with HF and LVEF ≤45%.

name: COPERNICUS — Carvedilol in Severe Heart Failure
one_liner: Carvedilol reduced mortality by 35% in severe HF (EF <25%, NYHA IV), proving beta-blockers safe even in decompensated HF.

name: MERIT-HF — Metoprolol Succinate in Heart Failure
one_liner: Metoprolol succinate reduced all-cause mortality by 34% in symptomatic HFrEF (NYHA II-IV, EF <40%).

name: VICTORIA — Vericiguat in Worsening Heart Failure
one_liner: Vericiguat (soluble guanylate cyclase stimulator) reduced CV death or HF hospitalization in recently worsening HFrEF.

name: STRONG-HF — Rapid Up-Titration After Acute HF
one_liner: Rapid up-titration of GDMT before discharge reduced 180-day HF readmission or death vs usual care after acute HF.

name: SELECT — Semaglutide and Cardiovascular Outcomes in Obesity
one_liner: Semaglutide 2.4 mg weekly reduced MACE by 20% in overweight/obese adults without diabetes with established CVD.

name: FOURIER — Evolocumab (PCSK9 inhibitor) in Atherosclerotic CVD
one_liner: Evolocumab reduced LDL by 59% and MACE by 15% in patients with atherosclerotic CVD on statin therapy.

name: CLEAR Outcomes — Bempedoic Acid in Statin-Intolerant Patients
one_liner: Bempedoic acid reduced MACE by 13% in statin-intolerant patients with or at high risk for CVD.

name: REDUCE-IT — Icosapent Ethyl in Hypertriglyceridemia
one_liner: Icosapent ethyl (EPA) reduced MACE by 25% in statin-treated patients with elevated triglycerides.

name: VOYAGER PAD — Rivaroxaban in Peripheral Artery Disease
one_liner: Low-dose rivaroxaban plus aspirin reduced major adverse limb and CV events after peripheral revascularization.

name: DAPT Study — Duration of Dual Antiplatelet Therapy After PCI
one_liner: Extended DAPT (30 months vs 12) after DES reduced stent thrombosis and MI but increased bleeding.

name: ACORN — Cefepime vs Piperacillin-Tazobactam in Suspected Sepsis
one_liner: No difference in 14-day outcomes between cefepime and pip-tazo for suspected sepsis; cefepime associated with more neurotoxicity.

name: MERINO — Piperacillin-Tazobactam vs Meropenem for ESBL E. coli
one_liner: Piperacillin-tazobactam was inferior to meropenem for definitive treatment of ESBL E. coli and K. pneumoniae bloodstream infections.

name: ProACT — Procalcitonin-Guided Antibiotics in Lower RTI
one_liner: Procalcitonin-guided antibiotic prescribing did not reduce antibiotic use in suspected lower respiratory tract infections in the ED.

name: POET — Partial Oral Treatment of Endocarditis
one_liner: Switching to oral antibiotics after initial IV in left-sided endocarditis was noninferior to completing full IV course.

name: OVIVA — Oral vs IV Antibiotics for Bone and Joint Infections
one_liner: Oral antibiotics were noninferior to IV for first 6 weeks of treatment in bone and joint infections.

name: ACTT-1 — Remdesivir in COVID-19
one_liner: Remdesivir shortened time to recovery by 5 days (10 vs 15) in hospitalized COVID-19 patients requiring supplemental oxygen.

name: RECOVERY — Tocilizumab in COVID-19
one_liner: Tocilizumab reduced 28-day mortality and ICU admission in hospitalized COVID-19 patients with hypoxia and systemic inflammation.

name: SOLIDARITY — WHO Repurposed Antivirals in COVID-19
one_liner: Remdesivir, hydroxychloroquine, lopinavir, and interferon-beta had little or no effect on hospitalized COVID-19 mortality.

name: SNAP — Short vs Standard Course Antibiotics for Bloodstream Infections
one_liner: 7-day antibiotic treatment was noninferior to 14 days for uncomplicated gram-negative bloodstream infections.

name: BALANCE — Short vs Long Antibiotics for Gram-Negative Bacteremia
one_liner: 7 days of antibiotics was noninferior to 14 days for gram-negative bloodstream infections with adequate source control.

name: TORCH — Salmeterol/Fluticasone in COPD
one_liner: ICS/LABA combination reduced exacerbations and improved lung function in COPD but did not significantly reduce mortality.

name: UPLIFT — Tiotropium in COPD
one_liner: Tiotropium reduced COPD exacerbations and improved quality of life but did not slow rate of FEV1 decline.

name: FLAME — LABA/LAMA vs LABA/ICS in COPD
one_liner: Indacaterol/glycopyrronium was superior to salmeterol/fluticasone in preventing COPD exacerbations with less pneumonia.

name: IMPACT — Triple Therapy in COPD
one_liner: Triple therapy (ICS/LAMA/LABA) reduced exacerbations vs dual therapy in symptomatic COPD with exacerbation history.

name: ETHOS — Budesonide/Glycopyrrolate/Formoterol in COPD
one_liner: Triple therapy with budesonide 320 mcg reduced exacerbations and all-cause mortality vs dual bronchodilator in COPD.

name: LOTT — Long-Term Oxygen Treatment in Moderate Hypoxemia COPD
one_liner: Supplemental oxygen did not improve survival, hospitalization, or quality of life in stable COPD with moderate resting or exercise desaturation.

name: INPULSIS — Nintedanib in IPF
one_liner: Nintedanib reduced the annual rate of FVC decline by approximately 50% in patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

name: ASCEND — Pirfenidone in IPF
one_liner: Pirfenidone reduced disease progression (FVC decline and 6MWD decline) vs placebo in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

name: PANTHER-IPF — Triple Therapy in IPF
one_liner: Prednisone + azathioprine + NAC increased mortality and hospitalizations vs placebo in IPF — establishing that immunosuppression is harmful.

name: INBUILD — Nintedanib in Progressive Fibrosing ILD
one_liner: Nintedanib slowed FVC decline in patients with chronic progressive fibrosing ILDs other than IPF.

name: CREDENCE — Canagliflozin in Diabetic Kidney Disease
one_liner: Canagliflozin reduced the renal composite endpoint by 30% in T2DM with albuminuric CKD (eGFR 30-90), stopped early for efficacy.

name: DAPA-CKD — Dapagliflozin in CKD (With and Without Diabetes)
one_liner: Dapagliflozin reduced sustained eGFR decline, ESKD, and renal/CV death by 39% in CKD regardless of diabetes status.

name: EMPA-KIDNEY — Empagliflozin in CKD
one_liner: Empagliflozin reduced CKD progression or CV death by 28% across a broad range of CKD, including lower proteinuria levels.

name: STOP-ACEi — Stopping RAS Inhibitors in Advanced CKD
one_liner: Discontinuing RAS inhibitors in advanced CKD (eGFR <30) did not improve or stabilize eGFR vs continuing them.

name: IDEAL — Timing of Dialysis Initiation
one_liner: Early initiation of dialysis (eGFR 10-14) did not improve survival vs late initiation (eGFR 5-7) in advanced CKD.

name: TREAT — Darbepoetin Alfa in CKD with Diabetes
one_liner: Darbepoetin targeting Hb 13 g/dL did not reduce death or CV events and increased stroke risk in diabetic CKD.

name: Villanueva — Restrictive Transfusion in Upper GI Bleeding
one_liner: Restrictive transfusion (Hb <7) improved survival vs liberal (Hb <9) in acute upper GI bleeding.

name: HALT-IT — Tranexamic Acid in GI Bleeding
one_liner: Tranexamic acid did not reduce death in GI bleeding and increased venous thromboembolic events.

name: PEPTIC — PPI vs H2RA for ICU Stress Ulcer Prophylaxis
one_liner: PPIs and H2RAs were similar for upper GI bleeding in ICU; PPIs associated with a trend toward higher mortality (not significant).

name: SUP-ICU — Pantoprazole vs Placebo for Stress Ulcer Prophylaxis
one_liner: Pantoprazole did not reduce 90-day mortality vs placebo for stress ulcer prophylaxis in ICU patients at risk for GI bleeding.

name: CALM — Tight Control in Crohn's Disease
one_liner: Biomarker-driven tight control with adalimumab escalation achieved better mucosal healing than symptom-driven management in Crohn's disease.

name: UNIFI — Ustekinumab in Ulcerative Colitis
one_liner: Ustekinumab was effective for inducing and maintaining remission in moderate-to-severe UC refractory to conventional or biologic therapy.

name: CLOT — Dalteparin vs Oral Anticoagulant in Cancer VTE
one_liner: Dalteparin reduced VTE recurrence by 52% vs oral anticoagulation in cancer patients, establishing LMWH as standard in cancer VTE.

name: Hokusai VTE Cancer — Edoxaban in Cancer-Associated VTE
one_liner: Edoxaban was noninferior to dalteparin for cancer VTE but increased major bleeding, especially GI with GI malignancies.

name: Caravaggio — Apixaban in Cancer-Associated VTE
one_liner: Apixaban was noninferior to dalteparin for cancer VTE without increased major bleeding, including GI bleeding.

name: EINSTEIN-DVT — Rivaroxaban for DVT Treatment
one_liner: Rivaroxaban monotherapy was noninferior to standard enoxaparin/VKA for acute DVT treatment with similar bleeding.

name: AMPLIFY — Apixaban for Acute VTE Treatment
one_liner: Apixaban was noninferior to standard therapy for acute VTE with 69% less major bleeding.

name: RE-COVER — Dabigatran for Acute VTE Treatment
one_liner: Dabigatran (after initial parenteral therapy) was noninferior to warfarin for acute VTE treatment with less bleeding.

name: PREVENT — Apixaban for Extended VTE Prophylaxis in Medically Ill
one_liner: Apixaban 2.5 mg BID for 30 days post-discharge reduced VTE by 64% vs placebo in medically ill patients without excess bleeding.

name: CASSINI — Rivaroxaban for Cancer-Associated VTE Prophylaxis
one_liner: Rivaroxaban VTE prophylaxis in ambulatory cancer patients reduced VTE on treatment but not in the overall ITT analysis.

name: AVERT — Apixaban for Cancer-Associated VTE Prophylaxis
one_liner: Apixaban reduced VTE in ambulatory cancer patients receiving chemotherapy but with a higher rate of major bleeding.

name: DCCT — Diabetes Control and Complications Trial
one_liner: Intensive insulin therapy (HbA1c ~7%) reduced microvascular complications by 50-76% vs conventional therapy in T1DM.

name: UKPDS — UK Prospective Diabetes Study
one_liner: Intensive glycemic control reduced microvascular complications by 25% in newly diagnosed T2DM; metformin reduced mortality in overweight patients.

name: ACCORD — Action to Control Cardiovascular Risk in Diabetes
one_liner: Targeting HbA1c <6% increased mortality vs standard (<7.5%) in T2DM with high CV risk, leading to early termination.

name: ADVANCE — Intensive Glucose Control in T2DM
one_liner: Intensive glucose control (HbA1c 6.5%) reduced nephropathy by 21% but did not reduce macrovascular events or mortality in T2DM.

name: EMPA-REG OUTCOME — Empagliflozin CV Outcomes in T2DM
one_liner: Empagliflozin reduced CV death by 38% and all-cause mortality by 32% in T2DM with established CVD — first SGLT2i CV benefit.

name: CANVAS — Canagliflozin CV Outcomes in T2DM
one_liner: Canagliflozin reduced MACE by 14% in T2DM with high CV risk but increased amputation risk.

name: DECLARE-TIMI 58 — Dapagliflozin CV Outcomes in T2DM
one_liner: Dapagliflozin reduced HF hospitalization by 27% in a broad T2DM population but did not significantly reduce MACE.

name: LEADER — Liraglutide CV Outcomes in T2DM
one_liner: Liraglutide reduced MACE by 13% and CV death by 22% in T2DM with high CV risk — first GLP-1 RA CV outcome trial.

name: SUSTAIN-6 — Semaglutide CV Outcomes in T2DM
one_liner: Semaglutide reduced MACE by 26% in T2DM with established CVD, driven largely by a 39% reduction in nonfatal stroke.

name: STEP 1 — Semaglutide 2.4 mg for Obesity
one_liner: Semaglutide 2.4 mg weekly produced 14.9% mean body weight loss vs 2.4% with placebo in adults with obesity.

name: SURMOUNT-1 — Tirzepatide for Obesity
one_liner: Tirzepatide produced up to 22.5% mean weight loss at the highest dose in adults with obesity without diabetes.

name: SURPASS-2 — Tirzepatide vs Semaglutide in T2DM
one_liner: Tirzepatide was superior to semaglutide 1 mg for HbA1c reduction and weight loss in T2DM.

name: NINDS tPA — IV tPA for Acute Ischemic Stroke
one_liner: IV tPA within 3 hours of stroke onset improved functional outcome at 3 months (NNT 8 for favorable outcome).

name: ECASS-III — Extended Window IV tPA for Stroke
one_liner: IV alteplase given 3-4.5 hours after stroke onset improved functional outcomes vs placebo (NNT 14).

name: MR CLEAN — Endovascular Treatment for Acute Ischemic Stroke
one_liner: Intra-arterial treatment within 6 hours improved functional outcomes in acute ischemic stroke with proximal anterior circulation occlusion.

name: DAWN — Thrombectomy 6-24 Hours After Stroke Onset
one_liner: Thrombectomy 6-24 hours after stroke onset improved outcomes in patients with clinical-core mismatch (NNT 2.8 for functional independence).

name: DEFUSE-3 — Thrombectomy 6-16 Hours After Stroke
one_liner: Thrombectomy 6-16 hours after stroke onset improved functional outcomes in anterior circulation LVO with perfusion mismatch.

name: CHANCE — Clopidogrel + Aspirin in Minor Stroke/TIA
one_liner: Dual antiplatelet (clopidogrel + aspirin for 21 days then clopidogrel alone) reduced stroke recurrence by 32% after minor stroke/TIA.

name: POINT — Clopidogrel + Aspirin in Minor Stroke/TIA
one_liner: Clopidogrel + aspirin reduced ischemic events by 25% within 90 days of minor stroke/TIA but increased major hemorrhage.

name: RESTART — Restarting Antiplatelet After ICH
one_liner: Restarting antiplatelet therapy after spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage did not increase recurrent ICH and may reduce vascular events.

name: PATCH — Platelet Transfusion in Intracerebral Hemorrhage
one_liner: Platelet transfusion in antiplatelet-associated ICH worsened outcomes vs standard care, shifting practice away from routine transfusion.

name: SPS3 — Blood Pressure Targets in Lacunar Stroke
one_liner: Targeting SBP <130 vs 130-149 mmHg showed a non-significant trend toward reduced recurrent stroke after lacunar infarction.

name: YEARS — Simplified PE Diagnostic Algorithm
one_liner: The YEARS algorithm (3 clinical items + age-adjusted D-dimer) safely reduced CT angiography use by 14% in suspected PE.

name: PEGeD — Age-Adjusted D-dimer for PE Diagnosis
one_liner: Age-adjusted D-dimer threshold (age x 10 for patients >50) safely excluded PE in 11.6% more elderly patients vs fixed 500 threshold.

name: Christopher Study — DVT Diagnostic Algorithm
one_liner: Wells score + D-dimer safely excluded DVT in outpatients: 3-month VTE rate of 0.5% in those ruled out.

name: CRASH-2 — Tranexamic Acid in Bleeding Trauma
one_liner: Tranexamic acid given within 3 hours of injury reduced all-cause mortality by 14% in bleeding trauma patients.

name: CRASH-3 — Tranexamic Acid in Traumatic Brain Injury
one_liner: Tranexamic acid reduced head-injury death in mild-moderate TBI when given within 3 hours, with no benefit in severe TBI.

name: FOCUS — Transfusion Trigger in Hip Fracture
one_liner: Liberal transfusion (Hb >10) did not reduce mortality or inability to walk vs restrictive (Hb <8 or symptomatic) in elderly hip fracture.

name: CLOTS 3 — Intermittent Pneumatic Compression in Stroke
one_liner: Intermittent pneumatic compression reduced DVT in immobile stroke patients (8.5% vs 12.1%) and is now standard of care.

name: MICHELLE — Rivaroxaban Extended Thromboprophylaxis Post-Hospitalization
one_liner: Rivaroxaban 10 mg for 35 days post-discharge reduced VTE by 67% vs placebo in high-risk medical patients.

name: ORAL Surveillance — Tofacitinib Safety vs TNF Inhibitors in RA
one_liner: Tofacitinib had higher rates of MACE, malignancy, and VTE vs adalimumab in RA patients with CV risk factors.

name: ESCAPE — Pulmonary Artery Catheter in Decompensated HF
one_liner: PAC-guided management did not improve outcomes vs clinical assessment alone in decompensated HF and increased adverse events.

name: SAVE — Captopril Post-MI with LV Dysfunction
one_liner: Captopril reduced mortality by 19% in asymptomatic patients with LV dysfunction (EF ≤40%) 3-16 days after MI.

name: VALIANT — Valsartan, Captopril, or Both Post-MI
one_liner: Valsartan was as effective as captopril in reducing mortality after MI complicated by HF/LV dysfunction; combination did not add benefit.

name: ONTARGET — Telmisartan, Ramipril, or Both in High-Risk Vascular Disease
one_liner: Telmisartan was noninferior to ramipril for CV events; combination increased adverse events without added benefit.

name: PARAGON-HF — Sacubitril/Valsartan in HFpEF
one_liner: Sacubitril/valsartan did not significantly reduce HF hospitalization/CV death vs valsartan in HFpEF (marginal miss), but subgroups with lower EF benefited.

name: GALACTIC-HF — Omecamtiv Mecarbil in HFrEF
one_liner: Omecamtiv mecarbil (cardiac myosin activator) modestly reduced HF events/CV death by 8% in symptomatic HFrEF.

name: SOLOIST-WHF — Sotagliflozin in Worsening Heart Failure
one_liner: Sotagliflozin initiated before or shortly after discharge for worsening HF reduced CV death and HF events by 33%.

name: SCORED — Sotagliflozin in T2DM with CKD
one_liner: Sotagliflozin (dual SGLT1/2 inhibitor) reduced CV death, HF hospitalization, and urgent HF visits by 26% in T2DM with CKD.

name: ATLAS ACS 2-TIMI 51 — Low-Dose Rivaroxaban Post-ACS
one_liner: Rivaroxaban 2.5 mg BID reduced MACE and CV death by 34% after ACS when added to dual antiplatelet therapy.

name: PEGASUS-TIMI 54 — Extended Ticagrelor Post-MI
one_liner: Ticagrelor 60 mg BID beyond 1 year post-MI reduced MACE by 16% vs placebo at cost of increased bleeding.

name: ODYSSEY Outcomes — Alirocumab After ACS
one_liner: Alirocumab (PCSK9 inhibitor) reduced MACE by 15% in high-risk post-ACS patients on maximally tolerated statin therapy.

name: SYNTAX — PCI vs CABG in Three-Vessel/Left Main CAD
one_liner: CABG was superior to PCI for complex three-vessel disease; PCI was reasonable for less complex anatomy (low SYNTAX score).

name: CHEST Guidelines — Antithrombotic Therapy for VTE Disease
one_liner: CHEST guidelines established DOACs as preferred over warfarin for VTE treatment and recommended extended therapy for unprovoked VTE.

name: REMAP-CAP — Anticoagulation in Hospitalized COVID-19
one_liner: Therapeutic heparin improved organ-support-free days in moderately ill COVID patients but was futile in critically ill ICU patients.

name: ORCHID — Hydroxychloroquine in Hospitalized COVID-19
one_liner: Hydroxychloroquine did not improve clinical outcomes vs placebo in hospitalized adults with COVID-19.

name: TOMAHAWK — Immediate Angiography After OHCA Without STEMI
one_liner: Immediate angiography did not improve 30-day survival vs delayed/selective approach after cardiac arrest without STEMI.

name: COACT — Immediate vs Delayed Coronary Angiography After OHCA
one_liner: Immediate coronary angiography did not improve 90-day survival vs delayed approach after OHCA without STEMI elevation.

name: WOMAN — Tranexamic Acid in Postpartum Hemorrhage
one_liner: Tranexamic acid reduced death due to bleeding in postpartum hemorrhage when given within 3 hours of delivery.

name: INTERACT2 — Intensive BP Lowering in Acute ICH
one_liner: Intensive SBP lowering to <140 mmHg within 6 hours of ICH onset improved functional outcomes without increasing adverse events.

name: ATACH-2 — Antihypertensive Treatment in Acute ICH
one_liner: Intensive SBP target of 110-139 vs 140-179 mmHg did not improve death or disability in acute ICH and increased renal adverse events.

name: ESCAPE-NA1 — Nerinetide in Endovascular Stroke Treatment
one_liner: Nerinetide (neuroprotectant) did not improve functional outcomes after endovascular thrombectomy for stroke (interaction with alteplase noted).

name: REWIND — Dulaglutide CV Outcomes in T2DM
one_liner: Dulaglutide reduced MACE by 12% in T2DM with and without established CVD over 5.4 years of follow-up.

name: VADT — Intensive Glucose Control in T2DM Veterans
one_liner: Intensive glycemic control (HbA1c 6.9% vs 8.4%) did not reduce major CV events in older T2DM veterans with long disease duration.

name: DPP — Diabetes Prevention Program
one_liner: Lifestyle intervention reduced T2DM incidence by 58% and metformin by 31% vs placebo in adults with prediabetes.

name: Steno-2 — Multifactorial Intervention in T2DM
one_liner: Intensive multifactorial intervention (glucose, BP, lipids, behavior) reduced CV events by 53% in T2DM with microalbuminuria.

name: DAPA-CKD Extended Follow-up
one_liner: Extended follow-up confirmed sustained renal and CV benefits of dapagliflozin in CKD with and without diabetes beyond the original trial.

name: CLOTS 1 — Graduated Compression Stockings in Stroke
one_liner: Thigh-length graduated compression stockings did not reduce DVT after stroke and increased skin complications.

name: MARINER — Rivaroxaban Extended VTE Prophylaxis Post-Discharge
one_liner: Rivaroxaban 10 mg for 45 days post-discharge did not significantly reduce symptomatic VTE vs placebo in acutely ill medical patients.

name: APEX — Betrixaban Extended VTE Prophylaxis
one_liner: Extended betrixaban (35-42 days) reduced VTE vs standard-duration enoxaparin in acutely ill medical patients.

name: MATE — Metformin in Acute STEMI
one_liner: Metformin did not reduce infarct size or improve outcomes when added to standard treatment in acute STEMI patients without diabetes.

name: CIBIS-II — Bisoprolol in Heart Failure
one_liner: Bisoprolol reduced all-cause mortality by 34% in moderate-to-severe HFrEF (NYHA III-IV, EF ≤35%).

name: EPHESUS — Eplerenone Post-MI with LV Dysfunction
one_liner: Eplerenone reduced mortality by 15% in post-MI patients with LV dysfunction and HF symptoms.

name: SHIFT — Ivabradine in Heart Failure
one_liner: Ivabradine reduced HF hospitalization in patients with HFrEF, sinus rhythm, and HR ≥70 bpm on maximally tolerated beta-blocker.

name: ATMOSPHERE — Aliskiren in Heart Failure
one_liner: Aliskiren neither alone nor with enalapril improved outcomes vs enalapril alone in HFrEF — negative trial for direct renin inhibition in HF.

name: GUIDE-IT — Biomarker-Guided HF Therapy
one_liner: NT-proBNP-guided therapy did not improve outcomes vs usual care in high-risk HFrEF; both groups had similar medication doses.

name: DAPA-MI — Dapagliflozin After MI Without HF/Diabetes
one_liner: Dapagliflozin did not reduce the hierarchical composite of CV death, HF events, or MI in patients after MI without HF or diabetes.

name: EMPACT-MI — Empagliflozin After MI
one_liner: Empagliflozin did not reduce the composite of first HF hospitalization or death in patients after acute MI at risk for HF.

name: OSCAR — High-Frequency Oscillatory Ventilation in Adults
one_liner: HFOV did not reduce mortality vs conventional ventilation in adults with moderate-to-severe ARDS.

name: OSCILLATE — HFOV in Moderate-Severe ARDS
one_liner: HFOV increased mortality vs low-tidal-volume ventilation in moderate-severe ARDS, ending its use in adults.

name: ART — Open Lung Approach with Recruitment Maneuvers in ARDS
one_liner: Open lung strategy with aggressive recruitment maneuvers and high PEEP increased 28-day mortality vs low PEEP in moderate-severe ARDS.

name: PROTECT — Dalteparin vs UFH for VTE Prophylaxis in ICU
one_liner: Dalteparin was not superior to UFH for VTE prophylaxis in critically ill patients; PE rates similar, but dalteparin reduced proximal DVT.

name: HEAT-PPCI — Bivalirudin vs Heparin in Primary PCI
one_liner: UFH was superior to bivalirudin for MACE reduction in primary PCI for STEMI with less stent thrombosis.

name: HORIZONS-AMI — Bivalirudin in Primary PCI for STEMI
one_liner: Bivalirudin monotherapy reduced major bleeding vs heparin + GP IIb/IIIa in primary PCI but increased acute stent thrombosis.

name: BRIGHT — Bivalirudin in AMI Undergoing PCI
one_liner: Bivalirudin with extended infusion reduced net adverse events vs heparin alone or heparin + tirofiban in AMI PCI.

name: COMPLETE — Complete Revascularization in STEMI
one_liner: Complete revascularization of non-culprit lesions reduced CV death or MI by 26% vs culprit-only PCI in STEMI.

name: EMPULSE — Empagliflozin in Acute Decompensated HF
one_liner: Empagliflozin initiated during hospitalization for acute HF improved clinical benefit at 90 days regardless of EF or diabetes.

name: ADVOR — Acetazolamide in Acute Decompensated HF
one_liner: Acetazolamide added to loop diuretics increased decongestion success at 3 days in acute decompensated HF.

name: TRANSFORM-HF — Torsemide vs Furosemide After HF Hospitalization
one_liner: Torsemide did not reduce all-cause mortality vs furosemide after HF hospitalization, despite better bioavailability profile.

name: FIDELITY — Finerenone Pooled Analysis in DKD
one_liner: Pooled FIDELIO/FIGARO data showed finerenone reduced both kidney and CV composite outcomes by 14-23% in T2DM with CKD.

name: FIDELIO-DKD — Finerenone in Diabetic Kidney Disease
one_liner: Finerenone (nonsteroidal MRA) reduced kidney failure and eGFR decline by 18% in T2DM with CKD and albuminuria.

name: FIGARO-DKD — Finerenone CV Outcomes in DKD
one_liner: Finerenone reduced CV composite events by 13% in T2DM with CKD, driven primarily by reduction in HF hospitalization.
